title: Phyllostegia glabriuscula
taxonomy: Phyllostegia glabriuscula is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to Hawaii, specifically the islands of Kauai and Oahu. The classification of Phyllostegia glabriuscula is as follows: it belongs to the genus Phyllostegia, which consists of about 15 species, and is further classified as a member of the family Lamiaceae, subfamily Nepetoideae. This subfamily also includes other plants such as mint and basil. The plant is a perennial herb with small purple flowers.

Its leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat various health issues.
